attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""I-arch-011 #1268 PR-c — per-basket VERIFIED-COMPOSE with basket-id-bound verbatim fallback.
2	
3	The composition FLOW step 3 ("great writing, verified"): turn each claim BASKET into readable
4	prose WITHOUT fabricating. For every basket the abstractive WRITER (the existing generator-role
5	LLM — NO new model) drafts prose carrying ``[#ev:<id>:<a>-<b>]`` provenance tokens; each sentence
6	is then re-checked by the UNCHANGED deterministic ``strict_verify`` (``verify_sentence_provenance``)
7	against a BASKET-SCOPED evidence pool. A passing sentence is kept; a FAILING sentence — including
8	one that cites a DIFFERENT basket's evidence_id (absent from the basket-scoped pool, so it fails
9	closed — the Codex P1-2 anti-cross-claim contract) — FALLS BACK to THAT BASKET'S OWN verified
10	K-span (a verbatim quote from one of its isolated-``SUPPORTS`` members). If the basket has no
11	verified span at all, an honest insufficient-evidence disclosure is emitted. The result is NEVER
12	empty (the original FIX-K empty-section bug).
13	
14	FAITHFULNESS (by construction, the crown-jewel rules are untouched):
15	  * The WRITER only ORGANIZES + PHRASES already-verified spans; it can never license an unsupported
16	    claim — a fabrication fails ``strict_verify`` and degrades to the verbatim K-span (QUOTATION is
17	    the only faithful-BY-CONSTRUCTION form — survey arXiv:2508.15396).
18	  * ``strict_verify`` / NLI / 4-role / provenance are UNCHANGED; this module calls the production
19	    single-sentence verifier as-is and consumes its binary verdict — it adds NO gate, relaxes none.
20	  * The verbatim fallback is BASKET-ID-BOUND: a failed sentence can fall back ONLY to its own
21	    basket's verified span, never another basket's (P1-2). Enforced by scoping the verify pool AND
22	    the fallback span to the basket's own members.
23	  * Deterministic: the fallback K-span + sentence split are pure; only the writer is an LLM call,
24	    and it is the EXISTING generator-role invocation (governed by polaris_runtime_lock.yaml exactly
25	    like every other generation). NO new model, NO new slug, NO new resolver.
26	"""
27	from __future__ import annotations
28	
29	import logging
30	import os
31	import re
32	import time
33	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
34	
35	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
36	
37	# The section heading the verified-compose body renders under when it is the PRIMARY producer.
38	# (Re-exported from multi_section_generator so the harness + callers share one constant.)
39	_ENRICHMENT_TITLE = "Corroborated Findings"
40	
41	_VERIFIED_COMPOSE_ENV = "PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE"
42	
43	# I-beatboth-002 Fix 1 (F1-1) — multi-cited verified synthesis. Default-OFF (LAW VI): when this
44	# flag is unset/off the module is byte-identical to the single-basket per-basket producer above;
45	# ON, the caller MAY co-locate per-member-verified clauses from N corroborating baskets into ONE
46	# multi-cited synthesized sentence. The faithfulness contract is UNCHANGED — see
47	# ``compose_multicited_sentence`` for the per-clause (NOT whole-sentence) verify invariant.
48	_MULTICITED_COMPOSE_ENV = "PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE_MULTICITED"
49	
50	# 2026-07-12 (compose_fix->outline_agent SPEED MERGE, port of compose_fix fb12406) — INTRA-SECTION
51	# BASKET CONCURRENCY. The authoritative per-section producer ``_compose_section_per_basket`` is a
52	# SERIAL for-loop over baskets; each basket's expensive work (writer_fn lookup + verify_fn NLI-judge
53	# network call + optional redraft) blocks the single worker thread the section runs on, so cross-basket
54	# concurrency is 1 (the route_all 328-basket long pole). When this knob is >1 the EXPENSIVE, PURE
55	# per-basket compose (compose+verify+redraft, plus the companion/qualifier/distinct additive passes)
56	# runs in a bounded ORDER-PRESERVING ThreadPoolExecutor (the MAP), while the ORDER-SENSITIVE
57	# dedup/survivor selection (seen_spans / seen_texts / seen_numbers_by_footprint) stays STRICTLY SERIAL
58	# in original basket order (the REDUCE) — because survivor selection is order-dependent and
59	# parallelizing it would change the kept/dropped verdict set. DEFAULT 1 => the legacy serial loop runs
60	# UNCHANGED (byte-identical); a value >1 opts into the map-then-reduce path. Faithfulness engine
61	# untouched: the map only moves WHICH thread runs the SAME writer_fn/verify_fn; no gate is added,
62	# relaxed, or reordered. NOTE (port reconciliation): this worktree's REDUCE mirrors THIS file's exact
63	# subset+text dedup contract (no citation-union / text_to_out_idx path — that lived in compose_fix's
64	# newer serial loop), so the parallel path is verdict-identical to THIS worktree's serial loop.
65	_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS_ENV = "PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS"
66	
67	# I-deepfix-001 M6 — verified CROSS-SOURCE analytical synthesis. Default-OFF (LAW VI): when unset/off the
68	# section producer is byte-identical (no import, no call); ON, an ADDITIVE pass appends analytical
69	# sentences (two verified atoms joined by an engine-LICENSED connective) on top of the keep-all
70	# single-source units. The faithfulness engine is never touched — see ``cross_source_synthesis``.
71	_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS_ENV = "PG_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS"
72	
73	# I-deepfix-001 Wave-1a (#1344) — SYNTH_PRIMARY: compose-then-verify. Default-OFF (LAW VI): when unset/off
74	# ``_compose_one_basket`` is byte-identical (first-failure break + K-span glue + disclosure UNCHANGED);
75	# ON *and* the caller threads a group-capable re-draft writer, the writer drafts one coherent paragraph
76	# per basket FIRST, then the UNCHANGED verify_fn filters each sentence downstream, a bounded whole-
77	# paragraph repair loop re-drafts failing sentences up to ``PG_WRITER_REPAIR_MAX``, and on exhaustion the
78	# uncovered-fact K-span renders as a SEPARATE labeled disclosure paragraph (never mid-line glued). The
79	# faithfulness engine is NEVER touched — only which draft is submitted changes.
80	_SYNTH_PRIMARY_ENV = "PG_SYNTH_PRIMARY"
81	_WRITER_REPAIR_MAX_ENV = "PG_WRITER_REPAIR_MAX"
82	_WRITER_REPAIR_MAX_DEFAULT = 2  # bounded whole-paragraph re-draft attempts (LAW VI; clamp >= 0)
83	
84	# A provenance token: ``[#ev:<evidence_id>:<start>-<end>]`` (the same shape strict_verify parses).
85	_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]*\]")
86	# Resolved-span grammar for idx8 seen-span dedup: parse the ``(evidence_id, start, end)`` identity out
87	# of every provenance token so a byte-identical re-emission of the same span can be detected and dropped
88	# (faithfulness-neutral — same resolved span, no new claim).
89	_EV_SPAN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:(?P<ev_id>[A-Za-z0-9_]+):(?P<start>\d+)-(?P<end>\d+)\]")
90	
91	
92	def _resolved_spans(text: str) -> set[tuple[str, int, int]]:
93	    """The distinct resolved (evidence_id, start, end) provenance tuples a composed unit carries.
94	
95	    Empty set for a unit with no ``[#ev:...]`` token (such a unit is NEVER a seen-span drop candidate —
96	    zero tokens must not be a vacuous "all duplicate"). Identity is the RESOLVED span (ev_id+offsets),
97	    so it is stable under both the stub and the real writer (both emit the same token grammar)."""
98	    spans: set[tuple[str, int, int]] = set()
99	    for m in _EV_SPAN_RE.finditer(text or ""):
100	        try:
101	            spans.add((m.group("ev_id"), int(m.group("start")), int(m.group("end"))))
102	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
103	            continue
104	    return spans
105	
106	
107	# I-arch-011 #1269 B11 (compose-repetition) — a number-token signature for the carve-out below.
108	# Standalone integers / decimals (incl. a %/$ neighbour), used ONLY to decide whether a same-span
109	# restatement adds a NEW statistic (it survives) or is a pure reword (it collapses). Conservative:
110	# a single shared 800-char span can carry >1 distinct statistic, so a sentence that introduces a
111	# number absent from its kept same-footprint siblings is KEPT — never dropped as "duplicate".
112	_NUMBER_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\d+(?:[.,]\d+)*")
113	
114	
115	def _number_tokens(text: str) -> frozenset[str]:
116	    """The distinct number tokens in ``text`` AFTER stripping every ``[#ev:...]`` provenance token
117	    (so the span offsets in the token never count as content numbers). Empty for non-numeric prose."""
118	    stripped = _EV_TOKEN_RE.sub(" ", text or "")
119	    return frozenset(m.group(0) for m in _NUMBER_TOKEN_RE.finditer(stripped))
120	
121	
122	def _canon_number(tok: str) -> str:
123	    """Canonicalize a number token for the L2 distinct-fact novelty test — strip thousands-separator
124	    commas so ``"400,000"`` and ``"400000"`` compare equal. Any residual collision under this
125	    normalization can only make two DIFFERENT numbers look EQUAL, which can only cause the additive
126	    pass to SKIP a candidate (under-emit) — never to falsely surface a duplicate. So the normalization
127	    is duplication-safe by direction. Pure."""
128	    return (tok or "").replace(",", "")
129	
130	
131	def _bare_integer_numbers(text: str) -> set[str]:
132	    """The comma-normalized BARE-INTEGER number tokens in ``text`` — number tokens that are NEITHER
133	    decimals (contain a ``.``) NOR percent-expressed integers (immediately followed by ``%`` /
134	    ``percent``). These are the absolute counts, currency amounts, year/date data, and multipliers that
135	    the abstractive writer's numeric-completeness gate (``abstractive_writer.make_writer_verify_fn``
136	    P1-3) does NOT force into the one-sentence paraphrase — so they are the numbers a headline can DROP
137	    on the SUCCESS path, and thus the ONLY numbers the L2 additive pass triggers on. Decimals and
138	    percent-integers are SUBSTANTIVE (P1-3 forces them into the headline) and are owned by the
139	    companion-figure pass; excluding them here keeps this pass byte-identical on percent/decimal-only
140	    content and non-overlapping with companion-figure. Provenance ``[#ev:...]`` tokens are stripped
141	    first so span offsets never count as content numbers. Pure."""
142	    stripped = _EV_TOKEN_RE.sub(" ", text or "")
143	    out: set[str] = set()
144	    for m in _NUMBER_TOKEN_RE.finditer(stripped):
145	        tok = m.group(0)
146	        if "." in tok:
147	            continue  # decimal -> substantive (headline-forced) -> not a droppable bare integer
148	        tail = stripped[m.end():m.end() + 9].lstrip()
149	        if tail.startswith("%") or tail[:7].lower().startswith("percent"):
150	            continue  # percent-expressed integer -> substantive -> owned by the companion-figure pass
151	        out.add(_canon_number(tok))
152	    return out
153	
154	
155	def dedup_same_span_sentences(sentences: list) -> tuple[list, list]:
156	    """I-arch-011 #1269 B11 — collapse degenerate same-span restatements to ONE rendering per
157	    distinct resolved-span FOOTPRINT (the (ev_id,start,end) SET a sentence cites), keep-FIRST.
158	
159	    THE DEFECT this fixes: the legacy section producer emitted ONE verified span (e.g.
160	    ``brynjolfsson_genai_at_work:0-800``) as 18 near-identical sentences — 18x the SAME footprint,
161	    zero added breadth (canary autopsy B11 / §-1.1 DO_NOT_SHIP). The §-1.3 reading: repetition of the
162	    SAME span is NOT corroboration; corroboration is DISTINCT works (distinct ev_ids). This pass keeps
163	    one sentence per distinct footprint so breadth comes from distinct works, not from re-citing one
164	    span N times.
165	
166	    FAITHFULNESS (by construction): every dropped sentence cites a footprint already emitted by a
167	    KEPT sibling — it is an already-strict_verify-PASSED restatement of an already-rendered span, so
168	    removing it can never drop a claim that is not still present, and it NEVER touches strict_verify /
169	    NLI / 4-role / span-grounding (it runs AFTER them, on the kept list). It is CONSOLIDATE-keep-one,
170	    NOT a cap/thinner/target — there is no fixed N; the bound is content identity (one per footprint).
171	
172	    DISTINCT-WORK SAFETY: a different ev_id => a different footprint => never collapsed. Multi-source
173	    corroborators (NBER ev_228 + MIT ev_224, etc.) carry DIFFERENT ev_ids and can never be merged or
174	    dropped by this pass — it only ever collapses re-citations of the IDENTICAL span set.
175	
176	    NUMBER CARVE-OUT (conservative, advisor 2026-06-21): a same-footprint sentence that introduces a
177	    number token absent from ALL its kept same-footprint siblings is KEPT (a single 800-char span can
178	    state >1 statistic; do not lose a genuine second number). A pure reword (no new number) collapses.
179	
180	    Order-preserving, pure. A sentence with NO provenance token is NEVER a duplicate candidate (zero
181	    footprint must not be vacuously "all duplicate") — it is always kept. ``sentences`` may be any
182	    objects exposing a ``.sentence`` str (the production SentenceVerification) OR plain strings.
183	
184	    Returns ``(kept, dropped)`` — ``kept`` is the deduped list in input order; ``dropped`` is the
185	    collapsed redundant objects (the caller routes them into the existing dedup-redundant telemetry)."""
186	    kept: list = []
187	    dropped: list = []
188	    # footprint (frozenset of (ev_id,start,end)) -> union of number tokens already KEPT for it.
189	    seen_numbers_by_footprint: dict[frozenset, frozenset[str]] = {}
190	    for sv in (sentences or []):
191	        text = sv if isinstance(sv, str) else str(getattr(sv, "sentence", "") or "")
192	        footprint = frozenset(_resolved_spans(text))
193	        if not footprint:
194	            kept.append(sv)  # no provenance token -> never a same-span duplicate
195	            continue
196	        nums = _number_tokens(text)
197	        if footprint not in seen_numbers_by_footprint:
198	            # First sentence for this footprint — always keep; seed its number set.
199	            seen_numbers_by_footprint[footprint] = nums
200	            kept.append(sv)
201	            continue
202	        # A later sentence on the SAME footprint: keep ONLY if it adds a number absent from every
203	        # kept sibling on this footprint (a genuinely new statistic inside the shared span); else it
204	        # is a pure reword of an already-rendered span -> collapse (faithfulness-neutral).
205	        new_numbers = nums - seen_numbers_by_footprint[footprint]
206	        if new_numbers:
207	            seen_numbers_by_footprint[footprint] = seen_numbers_by_footprint[footprint] | nums
208	            kept.append(sv)
209	        else:
210	            dropped.append(sv)
211	    return kept, dropped
212	# Sentence split — terminal punctuation (incl. a closing provenance ``]``) + whitespace + a new
213	# sentence start. Fixed-width lookbehind. Mirrors the conservative splitter used elsewhere.
214	_SENT_SPLIT_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=[.!?\]])\s+(?=[A-Z0-9])")
215	
216	
217	def _verified_compose_enabled() -> bool:
218	    """PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE gate. DEFAULT-OFF => the caller keeps the legacy section prose path
219	    byte-identical; ON => verified-compose is the PRIMARY per-section prose producer."""
220	    return os.getenv(_VERIFIED_COMPOSE_ENV, "0").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
221	
222	
223	def _multicited_compose_enabled() -> bool:
224	    """PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE_MULTICITED gate (I-beatboth-002 F1-1). DEFAULT-OFF => the multi-cited
225	    synthesis producer is never invoked and the module behaves byte-identically to the single-basket
226	    path; ON => the caller MAY compose a multi-cited synthesized sentence via
227	    ``compose_multicited_sentence``. Independent of ``PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE`` so the multi-cited path
228	    is a SEPARATE, explicitly-opted increment (no implicit activation)."""
229	    return os.getenv(_MULTICITED_COMPOSE_ENV, "0").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
230	
231	
232	def _compose_basket_workers() -> int:
233	    """PG_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS gate (port of compose_fix fb12406). DEFAULT 1 => the legacy SERIAL
234	    per-basket loop runs unchanged (byte-identical). A value >1 caps the ThreadPoolExecutor that runs
235	    the expensive PURE per-basket compose (the MAP) while the order-sensitive dedup REDUCE stays serial.
236	    Parse failures / values <1 coerce to 1 (fail-safe to the serial path — a bad env var can never
237	    disable dedup or drop a basket)."""
238	    raw = os.getenv(_COMPOSE_BASKET_WORKERS_ENV, "1").strip()
239	    try:
240	        n = int(raw)
241	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
242	        return 1
243	    return n if n > 1 else 1
244	
245	
246	def _cross_source_synthesis_enabled() -> bool:
247	    """PG_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS gate (I-deepfix-001 M6; DEFAULT-ON per I-deepfix-001 cov C2).
248	
249	    ON (the new default) => after the per-basket units are built, ADDITIVELY append engine-LICENSED
250	    cross-source analytical sentences (compare / contrast / agreement / extension) on top of the
251	    keep-all single-source units, so the DRB-II analysis dimension fires on EVERY render path — not
252	    only when the benchmark slate force-pins the flag. Each appended sentence joins TWO already
253	    strict_verify-PASSED atoms with an engine-licensed connective and RE-PASSES the FROZEN
254	    strict_verify per clause; an UNLICENSED pair stays neutral (never a fabricated relation word) and
255	    a clause that fails re-verify is DROPPED — faithfulness NEVER relaxed.
256	
257	    Kill-switch (LAW VI): ``PG_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS=0`` (or false/off/no) => the producer is never
258	    invoked and the section producer is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the pre-cov legacy path. §-1.3: this is a
259	    WEIGHT/CONSOLIDATE analysis lever (more synthesized relations = honest emergent depth), never a
260	    cap / target / thinner."""
261	    return os.getenv(_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS_ENV, "1").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
262	
263	
264	def split_into_sentences(text: str) -> list[str]:
265	    """Split composed prose into sentence units for per-sentence strict_verify. Keeps each
266	    sentence's trailing provenance token(s) attached (the ``]`` lookbehind). Empty-safe."""
267	    if not text or not text.strip():
268	        return []
269	    return [s for s in _SENT_SPLIT_RE.split(text.strip()) if s.strip()]
270	
271	
272	def _basket_supports_members(basket: Any) -> list[Any]:
273	    """The basket's isolated-``SUPPORTS`` members, highest credibility weight first (the verbatim
274	    fallback prefers the strongest verified source). ``span_verdict == "SUPPORTS"`` is the binding
275	    isolated-verification result (NEVER the advisory clustered count)."""
276	    members = list(getattr(basket, "supporting_members", None) or [])
277	    supports = [m for m in members if str(getattr(m, "span_verdict", "") or "").upper() == "SUPPORTS"]

301	def _basket_scoped_pool(basket: Any, evidence_pool: dict) -> dict:
302	    """The verify pool scoped to THIS basket's member evidence_ids — using the GLOBAL rows (so the
303	    cited token OFFSETS stay anchored to the global ``evidence_pool[eid]`` exactly as downstream
304	    resolution/audit will read them; Codex core-gate P1-3). A citation to a source NOT in this
305	    basket is absent entirely (fails closed). The remaining P1-1 anti-cross-claim guarantee (a
306	    shared source backing DISTINCT baskets with DIFFERENT spans) is enforced SEPARATELY, AFTER
307	    verify, by ``_tokens_within_basket_regions`` — a sentence may pass strict_verify against the
308	    global row yet cite a span belonging to a DIFFERENT basket's claim; the region check rejects it."""
309	    # ISSUE #1279 P1#1 (tightening): scope to the basket's isolated-``SUPPORTS`` members ONLY, NEVER the
310	    # full ``supporting_members`` (which intentionally KEEPS unsupported members per Principle 2). An
311	    # UNSUPPORTED member's evidence_id MUST NOT enter the verify pool — else a writer citing it would
312	    # strict_verify-PASS against the unsupported span and render unverified text as verified. Strictly
313	    # MORE restrictive (fails closed); the K-span fallback already uses SUPPORTS-only members.
314	    own_ids = {str(getattr(m, "evidence_id", "") or "") for m in _basket_supports_members(basket)}
315	    own_ids.discard("")

319	def _member_global_span(member: Any, evidence_pool: dict) -> Optional[tuple]:
320	    """The member's verified span as GLOBAL offsets into ``evidence_pool[eid]`` — computed by
321	    LOCATING the member's ``direct_quote`` inside the global row's text, NOT from
322	    ``BasketMember.span`` (Codex core-gate iter-3 P1: ``_assemble_baskets`` populates ``span`` as
323	    a LOCAL ``(0, len(direct_quote))``, so the offset must be recovered against the real downstream
324	    row). Returns None when the member has no quote or the quote is not a substring of the global
325	    row (fail-closed: that member cannot define an acceptance region or a fallback span)."""
326	    eid = str(getattr(member, "evidence_id", "") or "")
327	    quote = str(getattr(member, "direct_quote", "") or "")
328	    if not eid or not quote:
329	        return None
330	    row = (evidence_pool or {}).get(eid) or {}
331	    haystack = str(row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("statement") or "")
332	    if not haystack:
333	        return None
334	    idx = haystack.find(quote)
335	    if idx < 0:
336	        return None
337	    return (idx, idx + len(quote))

340	def _basket_member_regions(basket: Any, evidence_pool: dict) -> dict:
341	    """``evidence_id -> list[(start, end)]`` of THIS basket's members' verified-span regions as
342	    GLOBAL offsets into ``evidence_pool`` (via ``_member_global_span``). The acceptance region: a
343	    composed sentence's cited token MUST land inside one of its own basket's member regions, else
344	    it is asserting a DIFFERENT basket's claim of a shared source (the 1-to-many cross-claim leak)."""
345	    # ISSUE #1279 P1#1 (tightening): the acceptance region is built from isolated-``SUPPORTS`` members
346	    # ONLY, NEVER the full ``supporting_members``. An UNSUPPORTED member's span MUST NOT define an
347	    # acceptance region — else a sentence citing that member's span would pass the region gate even
348	    # though the member is not a supporting source for the claim. Strictly MORE restrictive.
349	    regions: dict = {}
350	    for m in _basket_supports_members(basket):
351	        gspan = _member_global_span(m, evidence_pool)
352	        if gspan is None:
353	            continue
354	        regions.setdefault(str(getattr(m, "evidence_id", "") or ""), []).append(gspan)

359	    """True iff EVERY provenance token in ``sentence`` cites an evidence_id in this basket AND its
360	    [start, end] falls within one of that member's verified-span regions. A token outside the
361	    member's region (a shared source's OTHER-claim span) -> False -> the sentence is rejected and
362	    the basket falls back to its OWN K-span. A sentence with no token -> False (fail closed)."""
363	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import parse_provenance_tokens  # noqa: PLC0415
364	    toks = parse_provenance_tokens(sentence)
365	    if not toks:

371	        spans = regions.get(eid)
372	        if not spans or not any(s <= t_start and t_end <= e for (s, e) in spans):

468	# I-deepfix-001 (Codex #1335 gate P2): a closed set of common abbreviations whose trailing ``.``
469	# is NOT a sentence boundary. Matched against the lowercased alphabetic token IMMEDIATELY before
470	# the ``.`` (so "Fig." → "fig", "vs." → "vs", "No." → "no", "pp." → "pp", "et al." → "al"). The
471	# dotted-initialism forms (U.S., e.g., i.e.) are caught by the single-letter-preceded-by-a-dot rule
472	# in ``_preceding_token_is_abbreviation`` rather than by listing every chained letter. Refusing a
473	# boundary here can only EXTEND the snapped span (extend-only + bounded), so a rare false positive
474	# never truncates a span — it is faithfulness-safe by construction.
475	_KNOWN_ABBREVIATIONS = frozenset(
476	    {
477	        "fig", "figs", "dr", "mr", "mrs", "ms", "prof", "no", "nos", "pp", "vs",
478	        "al", "vol", "etc", "eg", "ie", "cf", "approx", "ca", "inc", "ltd", "co",

484	def _preceding_token_is_abbreviation(haystack: str, k: int) -> bool:
485	    """True iff the alphabetic token immediately before ``haystack[k]`` (a ``.``) is a known
486	    abbreviation (``Fig`` / ``Dr`` / ``No`` / ``pp`` / ``vs`` / ``al`` …) OR a single letter that is
487	    itself preceded by a ``.`` — the dotted-initialism shape of ``U.S.`` / ``e.g.`` / ``i.e.`` (so the
488	    final dot of the initialism is not read as a sentence end). A single capital that is NOT preceded
489	    by a dot ("…the grade was A.") stays a real terminator. Pure, oob-safe."""
490	    i = k - 1
491	    while i >= 0 and haystack[i].isalpha():
492	        i -= 1
493	    token = haystack[i + 1:k].lower()
494	    if not token:
495	        return False
496	    if token in _KNOWN_ABBREVIATIONS:
497	        return True
498	    # Single-letter token glued to a leading '.' → a chained initialism (U.S. / e.g. / i.e.).
499	    if len(token) == 1 and i >= 0 and haystack[i] == ".":
500	        return True

500	        return True
501	    return False

504	def _is_real_sentence_terminator(haystack: str, k: int, n: int) -> bool:
505	    """True iff ``haystack[k]`` actually ends a sentence. ``!``/``?`` always do. A ``.`` does ONLY
506	    when the next char (past a closing quote/paren) is whitespace / EOL / end-of-string — a ``.``
507	    glued to a DIGIT is a decimal point (e.g. ``3.75``) and a ``.`` glued to a letter is an
508	    abbreviation, neither of which ends a sentence. This stops the span-snap from truncating a
509	    number to its integer part (Codex #1335 gate P1: ``... to 3.75`` must never snap to ``... to 3.``).
510	    I-deepfix-001 (Codex #1335 gate P2): a ``.`` immediately after a known abbreviation token
511	    (Fig. / Dr. / U.S. / et al. / e.g. / i.e. / vs. / No. / pp.) is likewise NOT a terminator, so the
512	    span-snap can no longer stop after the abbreviation. Extend-only + faithfulness-safe."""
513	    if not (0 <= k < n):
514	        return False
515	    c = haystack[k]
516	    if c in "!?":
517	        return True
518	    if c != ".":
519	        return False
520	    j = k + 1
521	    if j < n and haystack[j] in "\"')]":
522	        j += 1
523	    if not (j >= n or haystack[j] in " \t\r\n"):
524	        return False
525	    # The dot looks terminal (whitespace/EOL follows) — but reject it when the preceding token is a
526	    # common abbreviation, so e.g. "see Fig. 4 for the trend" never snaps to end after "Fig.".
527	    if _preceding_token_is_abbreviation(haystack, k):
528	        return False
529	    return True

532	def _ends_at_sentence_boundary(haystack: str, end: int) -> bool:
533	    """True iff the cited span [.., end) already ends at a REAL sentence boundary — the last
534	    non-(space/close-quote/paren) char before ``end`` is a genuine terminal ``. ! ?`` (a decimal
535	    point or abbreviation dot does NOT count). Empty/oob-safe."""
536	    k = end - 1
537	    while k >= 0 and k < len(haystack) and haystack[k] in " \t\r\n\"')]":
538	        k -= 1
539	    return _is_real_sentence_terminator(haystack, k, len(haystack))

542	def _snap_span_end_to_sentence(haystack: str, start: int, end: int) -> int:
543	    """EXTEND ``end`` forward to just past the next REAL sentence terminator in ``haystack`` when the
544	    cited span ends mid-sentence. Extend-ONLY (never shrinks) -> the result is always a SUPERSET of the
545	    original span within the SAME row. Returns ``end`` unchanged when the span already ends at a
546	    boundary, when no REAL terminator is found within the bounded scan (avoid running to EOF), or on
547	    any out-of-range input (fail-safe no-op). A decimal point / abbreviation dot is NOT a terminator
548	    (Codex #1335 gate P1) so the snap can never truncate a number to its integer part."""
549	    if not haystack:
550	        return end
551	    n = len(haystack)
552	    if not (0 <= start < end <= n):
553	        return end
554	    if _ends_at_sentence_boundary(haystack, end):
555	        return end
556	    try:
557	        max_extend = int(os.getenv(_ENV_SNAP_MAX_EXTEND_CHARS, "").strip() or _DEFAULT_SNAP_MAX_EXTEND_CHARS)
558	    except ValueError:
559	        max_extend = _DEFAULT_SNAP_MAX_EXTEND_CHARS
560	    max_extend = max(1, max_extend)
561	    limit = min(n, end + max_extend)
562	    for i in range(end, limit):
563	        if _is_real_sentence_terminator(haystack, i, n):
564	            j = i + 1
565	            if j < n and haystack[j] in "\"')]":
566	                j += 1
567	            return j
568	    return end

572	# I-deepfix-006 F (#1376) — FULL-QUOTE-WINDOW SNAP (the P0 truncated-number fix).
573	#
574	# THE BUG (drb_72): a verified span byte-range whose START or END lands MID-VALUE (the extractor cut the
575	# member's direct_quote at "…exposure rises to 4" from "…46%", or at "6% of tasks" from "46%…") composes
576	# a garbled clause with a truncated number/word. This SNAPS both window ends against the evidence-pool
577	# row's FULL direct_quote at window-mining time, so a number/word is never cut mid-value.
578	#
579	# Extend-ONLY within the SAME row => a SUPERSET of the verified span => grounded by construction (the
580	# emitted text is a verbatim slice of the row; strict_verify re-checks the whole number is present in
581	# both sentence and span). Bounded so a pathological token can never swallow the row. Default-ON;
582	# PG_FULL_QUOTE_WINDOW_SNAP=0 restores byte-identical legacy behaviour.
583	_ENV_FULL_QUOTE_WINDOW_SNAP = "PG_FULL_QUOTE_WINDOW_SNAP"
584	# A whole "value" token: a number (with decimal / thousands separators + a trailing %) OR an alphabetic
585	# word. Matching these lets the snap recognise a mid-value cut ("46%", "3.75", "reinstatement").
586	_WHOLE_VALUE_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\d[\d.,]*%?|[^\W\d_]+")
587	_MAX_WHOLE_VALUE_EXTEND = 64  # cap the per-end extension so a runaway token can never swallow the row

596	def _snap_window_to_whole_value(haystack: str, start: int, end: int) -> "tuple[int, int]":
597	    """Snap ``(start, end)`` outward to whole-value token boundaries in ``haystack`` so neither end cuts
598	    a number or word mid-value. Extend-ONLY (the result is a SUPERSET within the same row); bounded by
599	    ``_MAX_WHOLE_VALUE_EXTEND`` per end; a no-op on out-of-range input. Pure."""
600	    n = len(haystack)
601	    if not haystack or not (0 <= start < end <= n):
602	        return start, end
603	    new_start, new_end = start, end
604	    for m in _WHOLE_VALUE_TOKEN_RE.finditer(haystack):
605	        ms, me = m.start(), m.end()
606	        if ms < start < me:
607	            new_start = min(new_start, ms)
608	        if ms < end < me:
609	            new_end = max(new_end, me)
610	        if ms >= end:
611	            break  # tokens are ordered; nothing past ``end`` can contain ``start`` or ``end``
612	    # Bound each extension so a pathological token can never swallow the row.
613	    if start - new_start > _MAX_WHOLE_VALUE_EXTEND:
614	        new_start = start
615	    if new_end - end > _MAX_WHOLE_VALUE_EXTEND:
616	        new_end = end
617	    return new_start, new_end

756	def _snap_cap_to_sibling_member(
757	    basket: Any, member: Any, haystack: str, start: int, end: int, evidence_pool: dict
758	) -> int:
759	    """finding #8: the max ``snap_end`` that does NOT cross into ANOTHER basket ``SUPPORTS`` member's
760	    OWN verified quote located within THIS member's row ``haystack``. PURE; shrink-only (>= ``end``).
761	
762	    THE BUG: a merged multi-source sentence inherited only the FIRST source's citation — the report
763	    cited ev_036's Philippine text under ev_051's number [13] while it is correctly [14] elsewhere.
764	    When two members' quotes co-occur in one fetched row (or a member carries two spans of the same
765	    source), the FORWARD sentence-boundary snap of the first member's span can extend PAST the second
766	    member's span start and emit that second span's text under the FIRST member's single ``[#ev]``
767	    token — one source's content mis-attributed to another's citation. Capping the snap at the nearest
768	    sibling-member span start keeps each carried-up span bound to its OWN evidence_id / ``[#ev]`` token
769	    (its own ``[N]``), so a sibling's span never resolves under the wrong number. Faithfulness-neutral:
770	    the cap can only SHRINK an over-extended snap; it never widens a span and never relaxes a verdict.
771	    Returns ``len(haystack)`` when no sibling span sits after ``end``."""
772	    this_eid = str(getattr(member, "evidence_id", "") or "")
773	    boundary = len(haystack)
774	    for other in _basket_supports_members(basket):
775	        if other is member:
776	            continue
777	        o_eid = str(getattr(other, "evidence_id", "") or "")
778	        if o_eid and o_eid == this_eid:
779	            # Same-source sibling: cap at ITS span start (offsets index the same row) if it is after
780	            # this member's verified span end.
781	            o_span = _member_global_span(other, evidence_pool)
782	            if o_span is not None and end <= o_span[0] < boundary:
783	                boundary = o_span[0]
784	            continue
785	        # Different-source sibling whose quote physically co-occurs in THIS member's row text: cap at
786	        # the first occurrence after `end` so the snap never swallows the sibling's span.
787	        o_quote = str(getattr(other, "direct_quote", "") or "").strip()
788	        if not o_quote:
789	            continue
790	        idx = haystack.find(o_quote, end)
791	        if idx != -1 and idx < boundary:
792	            boundary = idx
793	    return boundary

797	    """The basket-id-bound VERBATIM K-span fallback: a sentence built from the basket's own
798	    strongest isolated-``SUPPORTS`` member's verbatim ``direct_quote`` (the span it was verified

800	    strict_verify trivially (it IS the verified span). Returns None when the basket has no verified
801	    span resolvable in the pool (caller emits an insufficient-evidence disclosure instead).

803	    I-wire-017 (#1339) FIX R1: build the run's corpus-vocabulary allowlist ONCE from the evidence
804	    pool and pass it (+ ``require_sentence_form=True``) into the per-unit chrome/truncation screen so
805	    this PRODUCER path screens out mid-word span cuts and subjectless fragments before they ship —
806	    previously the screen ran without these, so those legs were inert and the render seam was the

809	    # I-deepfix-001 P1_chrome_gate (#1344): make the ALL-CHROME-basket drop LOUD. When a member
810	    # resolves a verified span but EVERY one of its sentence units is screened out as chrome, the
811	    # basket silently falls through to the insufficient-evidence disclosure. Count those events and
812	    # emit a run-log canary if the whole draft returns None so an all-chrome basket dropping to a
813	    # disclosure is VISIBLE, not silent. MEASUREMENT-ONLY — never promotes a unit, never a verdict.
814	    all_chrome_member_drops = 0
815	    for m in _basket_supports_members(basket):
816	        eid = str(getattr(m, "evidence_id", "") or "")
817	        quote = str(getattr(m, "direct_quote", "") or "").strip()
818	        gspan = _member_global_span(m, evidence_pool)
819	        if not eid or not quote or gspan is None:
820	            continue
821	        start, end = gspan
822	        # I-deepfix-001 FIX-D part 3 (#1335): if the verified span ends MID-SENTENCE (the extractor cut

825	        # construction (the widened token covers exactly the emitted text), never fabricates. When no
826	        # snap applies (snap_end == end) the path is byte-identical to legacy. Default-ON.
827	        snap_end = end
828	        span_text = quote
829	        row = (evidence_pool or {}).get(eid) or {}
830	        haystack = str(row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("statement") or "")

832	        # number/word is never cut mid-value (the P0 truncated-number fix). Extend-only within the same
833	        # row => a SUPERSET of the verified span; span_text is rebuilt from the widened slice so the
834	        # emitted text and the [#ev] token bounds always agree. Byte-identical when PG_FULL_QUOTE_WINDOW_

836	        if _full_quote_window_snap_enabled() and haystack:

838	            if (w_start, w_end) != (start, end) and 0 <= w_start < w_end <= len(haystack):
839	                start, end, snap_end = w_start, w_end, w_end
840	                span_text = haystack[start:end]
841	        if _snap_span_enabled():
842	            snap_end = _snap_span_end_to_sentence(haystack, start, end)
843	            # finding #8: never let the forward snap cross into a sibling member's span (which would
844	            # emit that sibling's text under THIS member's single [#ev] token / [N]).
845	            if _snap_member_boundary_enabled():
846	                snap_end = min(
847	                    snap_end,
848	                    _snap_cap_to_sibling_member(basket, m, haystack, start, end, evidence_pool),
849	                )
850	            if snap_end > end and 0 <= start < snap_end <= len(haystack):

860	        units = [u.strip() for u in (split_into_sentences(span_text) or [span_text]) if u.strip()]

888	            "%d member(s) resolved a verified span but ALL its sentence units screened as chrome; "
889	            "basket falls through to the insufficient-evidence disclosure: %.160s",
890	            all_chrome_member_drops,
891	            subject,
892	        )
893	    return None

1071	def _atomic_fact_key(unit: str) -> str:
1072	    """Normalized dedup key for an atomic-fact sentence unit: strip any provenance token, drop
1073	    punctuation, collapse whitespace, lowercase. Two units with the same key are the SAME atomic fact
1074	    (the corroboration is preserved as multi-citation / pool membership elsewhere — L2 only avoids
1075	    repeating the SENTENCE, it never drops a source). Conservative (near-exact): only a genuine repeat

1082	def build_multi_member_sentences(basket: Any, evidence_pool: dict, research_question: str = "") -> str:
1083	    """L2 DETERMINISTIC multi-fact writer (region-safe ``writer_fn`` form). Emits one verbatim-span
1084	    sentence per DISTINCT atomic fact across ALL of the basket's isolated-``SUPPORTS`` members, deduped
1085	    by ``_atomic_fact_key``. Each unit carries TIGHT per-unit global offsets inside its member's quote
1086	    (NO forward snap), so every emitted token lands strictly WITHIN that member's own region — it passes
1087	    the UNCHANGED ``_compose_one_basket`` strict_verify + P1-1 region gate exactly like
1088	    ``build_short_member_sentence``. Cut-mid-word units are screened by ``_compose_junk_screen``
1089	    (require_sentence_form); if EVERY unit screens out this returns "" so ``_compose_one_basket`` falls
1090	    to the SNAP-preserving K-span fallback (``build_verified_span_draft_multi``), which recovers a cut
1091	    quote — so L2 never REGRESSES the single-headline recall. When PG_SUBTOPIC_DECOMPOSITION is OFF this
1092	    is byte-identical to ``build_short_member_sentence`` (single headline)."""
1093	    if not _subtopic_decomposition_enabled():
1094	        return build_short_member_sentence(basket, evidence_pool, research_question=research_question)
1095	    known_words = _known_words_for_compose(evidence_pool)
1096	    # I-deepfix-003 (#1374) STEP 5: per-section off-topic span-screen context (None => OFF/empty
1097	    # question => byte-identical; fail-open; faithfulness-neutral).
1098	    screen_ctx = _prepare_compose_offtopic_screen(research_question, evidence_pool)
1099	    limit = _subtopic_max_facts()
1100	    seen: set[str] = set()
1101	    out: list[str] = []
1102	    for m in _basket_supports_members(basket):
1103	        eid = str(getattr(m, "evidence_id", "") or "")
1104	        quote = str(getattr(m, "direct_quote", "") or "").strip()
1105	        gspan = _member_global_span(m, evidence_pool)
1106	        if not eid or not quote or gspan is None:
1107	            continue
1108	        start, _end = gspan
1109	        units = [u.strip() for u in (split_into_sentences(quote) or [quote]) if u.strip()]
1110	        # INPUT HYGIENE (P1-4): drop crawl/social/masthead chrome + truncated fragments; keep real
1111	        # short sentences. require_sentence_form drops a mid-word cut fragment (no forward snap here).
1112	        units = [
1113	            u for u in units
1114	            if not _compose_junk_screen(u, known_words, require_sentence_form=True)
1115	        ]
1116	        # I-deepfix-003 (#1374) STEP 5: WITHHOLD a confidently-off-topic span of this on-topic source
1117	        # from citation (fail-open; source never dropped; faithfulness engine untouched).
1118	        units = _apply_compose_offtopic_screen(units, (evidence_pool or {}).get(eid), screen_ctx)
1119	        for u in units:
1120	            key = _atomic_fact_key(u)
1121	            if not key or key in seen:
1122	                continue
1123	            off = quote.find(u)
1124	            if off < 0:
1125	                continue
1126	            tok_start = start + off
1127	            tok_end = tok_start + len(u)
1128	            u_core = _strip_terminal_punct(u)
1129	            out.append(f"{u_core} [#ev:{eid}:{tok_start}-{tok_end}].")
1130	            seen.add(key)
1131	            if len(out) >= limit:

1143	    member yields a real verified unit (caller emits the insufficient-evidence disclosure). Each unit is

1148	    # question => byte-identical; fail-open; faithfulness-neutral).

1150	    limit = _subtopic_max_facts()

1155	        eid = str(getattr(m, "evidence_id", "") or "")
1156	        quote = str(getattr(m, "direct_quote", "") or "").strip()
1157	        gspan = _member_global_span(m, evidence_pool)
1158	        if not eid or not quote or gspan is None:
1159	            continue
1160	        start, end = gspan
1161	        # FIX-D extend-only SUPERSET span snap (mirrors build_verified_span_draft): complete a

1163	        # widened token covers exactly the emitted text => grounded by construction, never fabricates.
1164	        snap_end = end
1165	        span_text = quote

1167	            row = (evidence_pool or {}).get(eid) or {}

1171	            # multi-source sentence never emits one member's span under another's single [#ev] token.
1172	            if _snap_member_boundary_enabled():

1174	                    snap_end,

1181	        units = [
1182	            u for u in units
1183	            if not _compose_junk_screen(u, known_words, require_sentence_form=True)
1184	        ]
1185	        if units_before and not units:

1187	            continue

1189	        # from citation (fail-open; source never dropped; faithfulness engine untouched). Applied AFTER

1202	            if len(out) >= limit:

1204	        if len(out) >= limit:

1413	def partition_composed_disclosures(units: list) -> "tuple[list[str], list[str]]":
1414	    """Split a ``_compose_section_per_basket`` result into ``(real_units, degraded_disclosures)``.

1417	    ``[uncovered supporting evidence for: ...]`` labeled K-span) is HELD ASIDE so it NEVER enters the
1418	    strict_verify-bound draft (where ``_repair_untokened_draft`` could rebind it or ``strict_verify``
1419	    would drop it). Every other unit passes through UNCHANGED, byte-for-byte (the common case).
1420	
1421	    I-deepfix-001 Wave-1a (#1344): a SYNTH_PRIMARY exhaustion unit carries the verified authored body
1422	    AND a trailing labeled uncovered-fact K-span as SEPARATE ``\\n\\n`` paragraphs. When (and ONLY when) a
1423	    unit contains a ``\\n\\n`` paragraph break it is split per-paragraph so the body stays real prose and
1424	    the labeled K-span is routed aside — as its OWN ``\\n\\n`` disclosure paragraph. Existing units are
1425	    always ``" ".join(...)`` (no internal ``\\n\\n``), so the fast path below is taken for every existing
1426	    unit and the output is byte-identical when PG_SYNTH_PRIMARY / PG_DEGRADED_VERIFY_DISCLOSURE are OFF.
1427	    Order-stable."""

1429	    disclosures: list[str] = []
1430	    for unit in (units or []):
1431	        # Fast path (byte-identical to pre-Wave-1a): a unit with no paragraph break is classified whole.
1432	        if "\n\n" not in str(unit):
1433	            if _is_composed_disclosure_paragraph(unit):

1435	            else:
1436	                real.append(unit)
1437	            continue
1438	        # SYNTH_PRIMARY mixed unit: route each paragraph independently.
1439	        body_parts: list[str] = []
1440	        for para in str(unit).split("\n\n"):

2011	# ── I-beatboth-002 Fix 1 (F1-1) — multi-cited verified synthesis ──────────────────────────────────
2012	#
2013	# The DRB-II analysis dimension needs cross-source SYNTHESIS: ONE rendered sentence that carries
2014	# corroborating citations from MORE THAN ONE basket. The faithfulness-by-construction design (advisor
2015	# 2026-06-20, Codex BRIEF APPROVE):
2016	#
2017	#   * VERIFY PER CLAUSE, NEVER the whole multi-token sentence against one pool. A co-located sentence
2018	#     carries tokens from >=2 baskets; verifying it WHOLE against any single basket's scoped pool would
2019	#     fail-closed on the OTHER basket's token (its evidence_id is absent from that scoped pool) and
2020	#     artifactually REJECT genuine multi-cite. That false rejection is the exact trap that would make
2021	#     per-basket "look too tight" and tempt the (OUT-of-scope) union pool. The escape hatch is to verify
2022	#     EACH per-member clause against its OWN basket's scoped pool + own-region gate (the existing P1-2 /
2023	#     P1-1 contract, untouched) and ONLY THEN co-locate. Each token was already verified against its own
2024	#     basket; the co-location asserts NOTHING new.
2025	#   * NO emergent aggregate predicate. The clauses are joined by a SEMANTICALLY-NEUTRAL connective
2026	#     (``"; "``) with no quantifier ("consistently" / "most" / "studies show"). The relational-quantifier
2027	#     guard is F1-2 (DEFERRED); because this producer never emits an aggregate predicate, it is SAFE
2028	#     without F1-2 (the boundary contract — under-relaxing is safe; over-relaxing is the lethal direction).
2029	#   * JOIN CHARACTER. ``"; "`` + a lowercased continuation keeps the multi-token co-location as ONE
2030	#     sentence under the production ``_SENT_SPLIT_RE`` (terminal ``.!?]`` + whitespace + ``[A-Z0-9]``);
2031	#     joining with ``". "`` would re-split the clauses apart and defeat the multi-cited requirement.
2032	#   * P1-2 FAIL-CLOSED preserved. A clause whose writer cites an evidence_id OUTSIDE its own basket
2033	#     fails closed under that basket's scoped pool and falls back to the basket's OWN verbatim K-span —
2034	#     a foreign citation can NEVER leak into the co-located sentence.
2035	#
2036	# strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding are UNCHANGED and run as-is per clause.
2037

2041	    with ``"; "`` as ONE multi-token sentence. A clause ending in a provenance ``]`` (or any other
2042	    char) is returned unchanged — the connective is appended after the token by the caller."""
2043	    clause = (clause or "").rstrip()
2044	    return clause[:-1].rstrip() if clause[-1:] in ".!?" else clause

2078	def _lowercase_first_alpha(text: str) -> str:
2079	    """Lowercase the FIRST alphabetic character of a continuation clause so the co-located sentence
2080	    does not present a mid-sentence capital that (a) reads as a new sentence and (b) trips the
2081	    ``[A-Z0-9]`` lookahead of ``_SENT_SPLIT_RE``. Proper-noun casing further into the clause is left
2082	    intact (only the leading word's first letter is touched). No-op on empty / non-alpha-leading text."""
2083	    for i, ch in enumerate(text):
2084	        if ch.isalpha():
2085	            return text[:i] + ch.lower() + text[i + 1:]
2086	        if ch.isalnum():

2091	def _join_verified_clauses(clauses: list, *, connective: str = "; ") -> Optional[str]:
2092	    """Co-locate N already-strict_verify-PASSED single-source clauses into ONE multi-cited sentence.
2093	
2094	    Extracted (I-beatboth-011 keystone-F1 F1-2b, #1284) from ``compose_multicited_sentence`` so BOTH
2095	    the cross-basket producer AND the within-basket producer share ONE join contract. Each input clause
2096	    is a string that ALREADY carries its own ``[#ev:<id>:<a>-<b>]`` provenance token(s) and ALREADY
2097	    passed the UNCHANGED ``strict_verify`` against its own span (the caller is responsible for that —
2098	    this helper NEVER verifies, NEVER relaxes a gate, NEVER edits a clause's claim; it only joins).
2099	
2100	    The clauses are joined by a SEMANTICALLY-NEUTRAL ``connective`` (default ``"; "``) with each
2101	    continuation lowercased at its first alpha char, so the result stays ONE sentence under the
2102	    production ``_SENT_SPLIT_RE`` (terminal ``.!?]`` + whitespace + ``[A-Z0-9]``) and asserts NO
2103	    emergent aggregate predicate — each clause keeps its OWN token, so the joined sentence still passes
2104	    ``strict_verify`` PER CLAUSE exactly as each clause did alone. Joining with ``". "`` would re-split
2105	    the clauses and defeat the multi-cited requirement; ``"; "`` + a lowercased continuation keeps the
2106	    co-location as one sentence (the join-char invariant).
2107	
2108	    Returns ``None`` when FEWER THAN TWO non-empty clauses are supplied (a single clause is not a
2109	    multi-cited synthesis — the caller keeps the unchanged single-cite path). Pure; order-preserving.
2110	    """
2111	    clean = [str(c).strip() for c in (clauses or []) if c and str(c).strip()]
2112	    if len(clean) < 2:
2113	        return None
2114	    # Terminal-strip the FIRST clause the SAME way as every continuation
2115	    # (``_strip_terminal_punct`` below) so a first clause ending in a sentence
2116	    # terminal (e.g. ``...wages.``) does not glue to the ``"; "`` connective as
2117	    # a ``".;"`` double-punctuation. A first clause ending in a provenance ``]``
2118	    # (or any non-terminal char) is returned unchanged by the helper, so the
2119	    # connective still lands right after the token. First clause keeps its
2120	    # leading capital (it opens the sentence) — only continuations are lowercased.
2121	    parts = [_strip_terminal_punct(clean[0])]
2122	    for clause in clean[1:]:
2123	        cont = _strip_terminal_punct(clause).lstrip()
2124	        cont = _lowercase_first_alpha(cont)
2125	        # Append the connective to the PREVIOUS part (after its provenance token), then the
2126	        # continuation — the splitter sees ``...] ; <lower>...`` and keeps it as one sentence.
2127	        parts[-1] = parts[-1].rstrip()
2128	        parts.append(cont)
2129	    sentence = connective.join(parts)
2130	    if sentence and sentence[-1:] not in ".!?]":
2131	        sentence = sentence + "."
2132	    return sentence

3251	    """True iff the basket has >=1 supporting member AND *EVERY* member row RESOLVES in the pool AND
3252	    every resolved row satisfies ``weighted_enrichment._is_confirmed_offtopic`` (the override-aware
3253	    SEMANTIC verdict). Precision-first: ANY member that is unresolved / missing from the pool / id-less
3254	    / unjudged / relevant / escalated_relevant PROTECTS the whole basket (returns False => KEEP).
3255	
3256	    Codex+Fable gate-fix P1-3: the prior impl looked ONLY at the RESOLVED subset of members, so a
3257	    basket with one resolved-off-topic member plus other MISSING members was wrongly withheld — it must
3258	    be KEPT (a missing member could be the real, on-topic corroborator). Withhold ONLY when the pool
3259	    confirms EVERY member is off-topic. Fail-open False (never withhold) on any import / attr / lookup
3260	    error."""
3261	    try:
3262	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.weighted_enrichment import (  # noqa: PLC0415
3263	            _is_confirmed_offtopic,
3264	        )
3265	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — recognizer unavailable => never withhold (fail-open)
3266	        return False

3269	        if members is None and isinstance(basket, dict):

3279	            row = evidence_pool.get(mid) if hasattr(evidence_pool, "get") else None

3363	#
3364	# THE LEAK (drb_72 ``no_provenance_token=34``): an abstractive-writer sentence that arrives with NO
3365	# ``[#ev:...]`` provenance token is silently DROPPED by strict_verify — so a claim genuinely supported

3367	# dropping, REPAIR it: bind the NEAREST supporting basket's OWN verified clause (via the per-basket
3368	# verified contract ``_per_basket_verified_clause``, :665) BEFORE strict_verify. The emitted clause is
3369	# that basket's own strict_verify-PASSED span carrying a real ``[#ev]`` token — faithful-BY-CONSTRUCTION,
3370	# exactly the QUOTATION-is-faithful philosophy the K-span fallback already uses everywhere in this module.
3371	#
3372	# §-1.3 / FAITHFULNESS: this NEVER fabricates a binding. It does NOT keep the untokened sentence and it
3373	# does NOT staple a token onto it; it REPLACES it with the nearest overlapping basket's verified clause.
3374	# "Nearest" = maximum shared content-word overlap between the sentence and the basket's claim + its
3375	# isolated-``SUPPORTS`` members' spans, requiring >= ``min_overlap`` (default 2, mirroring the
3376	# strict_verify >=2-content-word invariant) so an UNRELATED basket is never bound. If NO candidate basket
3377	# clears the overlap bar AND yields a verified clause, the sentence is STILL dropped (returns ``None``) —
3378	# the repair can only ADD a faithful cited clause where the legacy path rendered nothing; it can never
3379	# make the output less faithful. The frozen faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / provenance /
3380	# span-grounding) is UNTOUCHED: the returned clause re-passes the UNCHANGED strict_verify per clause.

3382	# drop) => byte-identical.

3384	_DEFAULT_REPAIR_MIN_OVERLAP = 2

3387	# The basket path above REPLACES an untokened sentence with the nearest basket's clause; it recovers a
3388	# sentence only when a CONSOLIDATED basket carries a matching isolated-SUPPORTS claim. The drb_72 leak
3389	# left ~15 GROUNDABLE quantitative findings untokened that NO basket bound (the composer wrote them
3390	# straight from a source span, but the consolidation produced no matching basket) — so the basket path
3391	# returns None and strict_verify drops them ``no_provenance_token`` despite the number + prose living
3392	# verbatim in a real evidence row. This fallback FINISHES the repair: for an untokened QUANTITATIVE
3393	# sentence, it finds the real evidence element_id + the char span the finding was written from, attaches
3394	# the correct ``[#ev:<id>:<start>-<end>]`` to the ORIGINAL sentence (PRESERVING the finding), and re-runs
3395	# the UNCHANGED ``verify_fn`` (the same per-clause strict_verify the compose paths use). The sentence is
3396	# kept ONLY IF that unchanged gate PASSES; else it stays dropped (never a fabricated binding). Default-ON;

3400	# MAX_SOURCES rows that pass the cheap decimal+overlap prefilter are searched, and at most MAX_CANDIDATES
3401	# decimal-containing candidate spans per row are verified.
3402	_SPAN_GROUNDING_MAX_SOURCES_ENV = "PG_NO_TOKEN_SPAN_GROUNDING_MAX_SOURCES"

3500	def ground_untokened_sentence_to_span(
3501	    sentence: str,
3502	    evidence_pool: dict,
3503	    *,
3504	    verify_fn: Callable[..., Any],
3505	    min_overlap: int = _DEFAULT_REPAIR_MIN_OVERLAP,
3506	) -> Optional[str]:
3507	    """I-deepfix-001 V3 (#1344) — DIRECT evidence-span grounding for an untokened QUANTITATIVE finding.
3508	
3509	    The FINISH of the no-provenance-token repair (the drb_72 ``no_provenance_token`` leak on the ~15
3510	    groundable quantitative findings the basket path could not bind). For an untokened sentence carrying
3511	    a NUMBER, search the evidence pool for the real source row + the char span (inside that row's
3512	    ``direct_quote`` / ``statement``) that the finding was written from: a span that (a) contains ALL the
3513	    sentence's decimals and (b) shares >= ``min_overlap`` content words. Attach the correct
3514	    ``[#ev:<id>:<start>-<end>]`` token to the ORIGINAL sentence (preserving the finding, never rewording
3515	    it) and re-run the UNCHANGED ``verify_fn`` per clause with ``allow_local_window_fallback=False`` so the
3516	    BOUND span must ITSELF entail. Return the tokened sentence ONLY IF that gate PASSES (decimal-in-span +
3517	    percent-role + qualifier + >=2 overlap + BOUND-SPAN-ONLY ENTAILMENT-enforce); else ``None`` (the
3518	    finding stays dropped — never a fabricated binding).
3519	
3520	    Returns ``None`` (no grounding) when: the fallback flag is OFF; the sentence already carries a token;
3521	    entailment is not in ``enforce`` mode (the search-for-a-match laundering guard); the sentence carries
3522	    no decimal (no numeric anchor -> not this fallback's scope); the sentence has < ``min_overlap``
3523	    content words; or NO span in any row verifies.
3524	
3525	    §-1.3 / FAITHFULNESS: the frozen faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / provenance / span-
3526	    grounding) is UNTOUCHED. This attaches a token to the sentence's OWN real span and keeps it ONLY when
3527	    the UNCHANGED gate confirms it — it can only recover a finding that already clears the full bar. A
3528	    genuinely ungroundable sentence is STILL dropped. Enforce-only + numeric-anchor bound the search so a
3529	    coincidental match can never launder a drop into an unverified pass.
3530	    """
3531	    if not no_token_span_grounding_enabled():
3532	        return None
3533	    if not sentence or _EV_TOKEN_RE.search(sentence):
3534	        return None
3535	    # Enforce-only accept gate (faithfulness-critical; mirrors _try_reanchor). Off/warn => no grounding.
3536	    if not _entailment_enforce_active():
3537	        return None
3538	    sentence_decimals = _repair_decimals(sentence)
3539	    if not sentence_decimals:
3540	        return None  # numeric-anchor scope: a sentence with no number is left to the basket path
3541	    sentence_words = _repair_content_words(sentence)
3542	    if len(sentence_words) < min_overlap:
3543	        return None
3544	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
3545	        _reanchor_candidate_spans,
3546	    )
3547	    max_sources = _span_grounding_max_sources()
3548	    max_candidates = _span_grounding_max_candidates()
3549	    # Place the token INLINE — before any trailing terminal punctuation — so the downstream
3550	    # ``strict_verify`` sentence splitter (terminal ``.!?]`` + whitespace) keeps the token WITH its
3551	    # sentence instead of splitting it into a tokenless fragment (which would re-drop it
3552	    # no_provenance_token). ``stem`` + ``terminal`` reassemble around the emitted token.
3553	    base = sentence.rstrip()
3554	    _term_match = re.search(r"[.!?]+$", base)
3555	    if _term_match:
3556	        stem = base[: _term_match.start()].rstrip()
3557	        terminal = _term_match.group(0)
3558	    else:
3559	        stem = base
3560	        terminal = ""
3561	    searched = 0
3562	    for evidence_id, ev in (evidence_pool or {}).items():
3563	        if not isinstance(ev, dict):
3564	            continue
3565	        direct_quote = ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or ""
3566	        if not direct_quote:
3567	            continue
3568	        # Cheap prefilter (no judge call): the row must carry EVERY sentence decimal and >= min_overlap
3569	        # shared content words, else no tight span in it can ground the finding.
3570	        if not sentence_decimals.issubset(_repair_decimals(direct_quote)):
3571	            continue
3572	        if len(sentence_words & _repair_content_words(direct_quote)) < min_overlap:
3573	            continue
3574	        searched += 1
3575	        if searched > max_sources:
3576	            break
3577	        # Only verify TIGHT candidate spans that themselves carry all the sentence's decimals (so the
3578	        # bound span — not a distant part of the row — supports the number). Bounded per row.
3579	        verified_candidates = 0
3580	        for (cand_start, cand_end) in _reanchor_candidate_spans(direct_quote):
3581	            if verified_candidates >= max_candidates:
3582	                break
3583	            if not sentence_decimals.issubset(_repair_decimals(direct_quote[cand_start:cand_end])):
3584	                continue
3585	            verified_candidates += 1
3586	            candidate = f"{stem} [#ev:{evidence_id}:{cand_start}-{cand_end}]{terminal}"
3587	            # I-deepfix-001 V3 iter-2 (#1344) — Codex P1 laundering-leak fix: force
3588	            # ``allow_local_window_fallback=False`` so the BOUND span ``[#ev:id:start-end]`` must
3589	            # ITSELF entail. Without it, ``verify_sentence_provenance`` defaults the flag ``True``
3590	            # (provenance_generator.py:2049) and a candidate whose bound span is NEUTRAL can still
3591	            # PASS via a DIFFERENT in-row local window — laundering an unverified binding through with
3592	            # its token pointing at a non-entailing span. Mirrors the enforce-only accept gate in
3593	            # ``provenance_generator._try_reanchor`` (:1573/:1625/:1663). Every production ``verify_fn``
3594	            # on this path accepts the kwarg: the bare ``verify_sentence_provenance`` takes it directly;
3595	            # the abstractive ``make_writer_verify_fn`` wrapper takes ``**kwargs`` and already
3596	            # ``setdefault``s it False. A ``verify_fn`` that REJECTS the kwarg raises ``TypeError`` ->
3597	            # caught below -> candidate SKIPPED (fail-closed; never a laundered default-True pass).
3598	            try:
3599	                res = verify_fn(candidate, evidence_pool, allow_local_window_fallback=False)
3600	            except Exception:  # pragma: no cover — a verify error / signature mismatch is a non-recovery, never a pass
3601	                continue
3602	            if bool(getattr(res, "is_verified", False)):
3603	                logger.info(
3604	                    "[verified_compose] V3 span-grounding: bound untokened quantitative finding to "
3605	                    "%s[%d-%d] (re-verified by the UNCHANGED strict_verify)",
3606	                    evidence_id, cand_start, cand_end,
3607	                )
3608	                return candidate
3609	    return None

3612	def repair_untokened_sentence(
3613	    sentence: str,
3614	    baskets: list,
3615	    evidence_pool: dict,
3616	    *,
3617	    writer_fn: Callable[[Any, dict], str],
3618	    verify_fn: Callable[..., Any],
3619	    min_overlap: int = _DEFAULT_REPAIR_MIN_OVERLAP,
3620	) -> Optional[str]:
3621	    """Repair an abstractive-writer sentence that carries NO provenance token by binding the NEAREST
3622	    supporting basket's verified clause (via ``_per_basket_verified_clause``), so its source counts as
3623	    breadth instead of being silently dropped.
3624	
3625	    Returns:
3626	      * the ORIGINAL ``sentence`` unchanged when it ALREADY carries a ``[#ev:...]`` token (only an
3627	        untokened sentence is a repair candidate — a tokened one is handled by the normal path);
3628	      * a verified, tokened clause (the nearest overlapping basket's OWN strict_verify-PASSED span)
3629	        when a real ``SUPPORTS`` span can be bound;
3630	      * ``None`` when the flag is OFF, the sentence is empty/wordless, or NO candidate basket clears the
3631	        overlap bar AND yields a verified clause — the sentence is then STILL dropped (never fabricate a
3632	        binding).
3633	
3634	    §-1.3: the untokened sentence is REPLACED by the bound basket's verified clause, never kept and
3635	    never given a fabricated token; the clause re-passes the UNCHANGED ``strict_verify`` per clause. The
3636	    caller supplies the production ``writer_fn`` / ``verify_fn`` (the same ones every compose path uses),
3637	    so the repair adds NO new model and touches NO gate.
3638	    """
3639	    if not sentence or not sentence.strip():
3640	        return None
3641	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 PART 2 ARM B P1a (#1344): a no-verified-span DISCLOSURE placeholder (the
3642	    # legacy insufficient-evidence gap OR the ARM-B degraded-verify label) is NOT a repair candidate —
3643	    # it must NEVER be rebound to a foreign SUPPORTS basket (that would launder an honest gap disclosure
3644	    # into a fabricated cited claim). Return it UNCHANGED (exactly as a tokened sentence is), so
3645	    # ``_repair_untokened_draft`` counts it as "not repaired" and leaves it as-is. Faithfulness: a
3646	    # disclosure is not a claim; rebinding it is the bug.
3647	    if _is_no_verified_span_disclosure(sentence):
3648	        return sentence
3649	    # A sentence that already cites a span is not this path's concern — return it untouched.
3650	    if _EV_TOKEN_RE.search(sentence):
3651	        return sentence
3652	    if not no_token_sentence_repair_enabled():
3653	        return None  # legacy: an untokened sentence is dropped
3654	    sentence_words = _repair_content_words(sentence)
3655	    if not sentence_words:
3656	        return None
